name: Ross Tompkins
abstract: Ross Tompkins (May 13, 1938 – June 30, 2006) was an American jazz pianist who was a member of The Tonight Show Band.

interviews.title: Tommy Newsom and Ross Tompkins interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

works.t: New Faces of 1952: Guess Who I Saw Today
Evita: Don’t Cry for Me, Argentina

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Watrous
about_quotes.evidence: And Ross Tompkins and Russell George said, “look I don’t care if he can read a note or not, he swings like crazy and we can teach him to read.” And sure enough, I got hired for the gig
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Watrous interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 26, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Frishberg
about_quotes.evidence: Ross Tompkins and Roger Kellaway and I maybe, were the three players that were used as house players.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Frishberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Candoli
about_quotes.evidence: I was talking to Ross Tompkins and we play this duo thing sometimes now and then

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Sheldon
about_quotes.evidence: And I work at Chadney’s every Sunday night with Ross Tompkins, we do a duet.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack Sheldon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Most
about_quotes.evidence: I had Ross Tompkins, Lawrence Marable, Jeff Hamilton. Pick-up groups.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sam Most interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Jake Hanna
about_quotes.evidence: Ross Tompkins knows him. ... Ross Tompkins’ father knew him in Detroit. ... Ross Tompkins got it all down, you know. ... Ross Tompkins or Teddy Wilson would always ask if he could have me you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jake Hanna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997
